other_findings: Lymphoid progenitors detected in first-trimester fetal skin by scRNAseq (Popescu et al., 2019)
Lymphocyte precursors were more abundant in embryonic than fetal skin
ILC precursors identified in fetal skin from 8 PCW
Lymphoid progenitors may differentiate in situ within the fetal dermis

evidence_quotes.quote: While T-cells were only detected in fetal skin after thymic production, natural killer (NK) cells and innate lymphoid cell (ILC) precursors have been identified in skin as early as 8 PCW.
evidence_quotes.context: ILC precursors detected in fetal skin from 8 PCW

evidence_quotes.quote: s well as ILC precursors, lymphocyte precursors were also identified in first-trimester skin by scRNAseq, where they were found to be more abundant in embryonic than fetal skin.
evidence_quotes.context: Lymphocyte precursors in first-trimester fetal skin

evidence_quotes.quote: The presence of megakaryocyte-erythrocyte-mast cell progenitors (MEMPs), lymphocyte precursors, B-cell progenitors, ILC precursors and their progeny 41 indicates differentiation of these lineages may be supported within developing dermis.
evidence_quotes.context: Multiple progenitor lineages differentiating in fetal dermis

evidence_quotes.quote: Innate immune cells, such as macrophages and innate lymphoid cells (ILCs), were present from early gestation, whereas B cells and T cells emerged later, accompanying thymus, bone marrow and spleen formation from around 10 PCW
evidence_quotes.context: Temporal immune cell composition in prenatal skin
